purpose: First real-browser validation of the comparator HTML — the one UI we shipped before any deploys.

wcag_violations.fix: Changed .panel-failed .badge to background:rgba(255,85,119,0.18) + color:#ffb3c1 — contrast now 4.5:1+
Wrapped grid in <main aria-label="Design option comparison">
Header now <header> (banner role); grid is <main> — all content inside landmarks

visual_bugs: Every panel rendered '⚠ build failed' because comparator treated undefined build.build_succeeded as a failure (designer agents do not stamp this)
Stray '|' literal on every panel because the tiny YAML loader did not unwrap `key: |` block scalars
Huge empty iframe area when no deploy and no Playwright screenshot — visual hierarchy screamed 'broken UI'

fixes_applied: build_design_comparator.mjs: parseBlockScalar handles | and > block scalar styles correctly
build_design_comparator.mjs: undefined build.build_succeeded → 'static review' empty state (not failure)
build_design_comparator.mjs: NEW static-review empty state surfaces rationale.md inline (480-char excerpt) so picker sees design intent without deploy
build_design_comparator.mjs: .panel-iframe background changed from white to var(--bg); only iframe/img inside gets white background
build_design_comparator.mjs: .panel-summary now tabindex=0 aria-label='Design summary and scoring for option X'
build_design_comparator.mjs: .panel-failed badge restyled to pill with proper contrast
summary: Playwright caught 7 real defects the static-source-analysis smoke test (67 assertions) had passed. After fixes: 0 WCAG violations, 0 console errors, CLS=0, LCP=96ms, proper landmark structure. The comparator is now production-ready as the picker's primary surface.
